author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xviii. 7, where we are admonished to beware of the offences of the world.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xviii. 7, where we are admonished to beware of the offences of the world.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The divine lessons, which we have just heard as they were being read, warn us to gather in a stock of virtues, to fortify a Christian heart, against the offences which were predicted to come, and this from the mercy of the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For what is man,” saith Scripture, “saving that Thou art mindful of him?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Woe unto the world because of offences,” saith the Lord; the Truth says so; He alarmeth and warneth us, He would not have us to be off our guard; for surely He would not make us desperate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Against this “woe,” against this evil, that is, which is to be feared, and dreaded, and guarded against, Scripture counsels, and exhorts, and instructs us in that place, where it is said, “Great praise have they who love Thy law, and nothing is an offence to them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath shown us an enemy to be guarded against, but He hath not omitted to show us also a wall of defence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wast thinking, as thou heardest, “Woe unto the world because of offences,” whither thou mightest go beyond the world, that thou mightest not be exposed to offences.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore to avoid offences, whither wilt thou go beyond the world, unless thou fly to Him who made the world?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how shall we be able to fly to Him who made the world, unless we give ear to His law which is preached everywhere?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And to give ear to it is but a small matter, unless we love it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For divine Scripture in making thee secure against offences doth not say, “Great peace have they who” hear “Thy law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For not the hearers of the law are just before God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But” because “the doers of the law shall be justified,” and, “faith worketh by love:” it saith, “Great peace have they who love Thy law, and nothing is an offence to them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To this sentiment also agrees the passage which we have chanted in course; “But the meek shall inherit the earth, and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because, “great peace have they who love Thy law.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For these “meek” ones are they who “love the law of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, “Blessed is the man whom Thou chastenest, O Lord, and teachest him out of Thy law, that Thou mayest give him rest from the days of adversity, until the pit be digged for the sinner.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How diverse seem those words of Scripture, yet into one meaning do they so flow and meet together, that whatsoever out of that most rich fountain thou canst hear, so that thou acquiesce therein, and art in loving harmony with the truth, thou will be at once filled with peace; glowing with love, and fortified against offences.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is our place then to see, or seek, or learn, how we must be “meek;” and we are guided by that which I have just brought forward out of the Scriptures, to find what we are in quest of.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be attentive then, Beloved, for a little while; it is a weighty matter that is in hand, that we may be meek; a necessary thing in the adversities of life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But it is not the adverse circumstances of this life which are called offences; but mark what “offences” are.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A man, for instance, under some hard necessity is weighed down by a press of trouble.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That he is weighed down with a press of trouble, is no offence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By such pressure were even Martyrs pressed, but not oppressed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of an offence beware, but of a press of trouble not so much.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The last presseth thee, an offence oppresseth thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is the difference between the two?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the press of trouble thou didst make ready to maintain patience, to hold fast constancy, not to abandon faith, not to consent to sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This if thou maintain, or shall have maintained, the trouble that presseth thee shall not be thy fall; but that press of trouble shall avail to the same end as in the oil press, not to destroy the olive, but to extract the oil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In a word, if in this trouble that presseth thee thou ascribe praise unto God, how useful will the press be to thee, whereby such oil is pressed out!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Under such a press the Apostles sat in chains, and in that press they sang a hymn to God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What precious oil was this that was pressed and forced out!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Beneath a heavy press did Job sit on the dunghill, without resource, without help, without substance, without children; full, but of worms only, as far, that is, as concerned the outward man, but because he too was full of God within, he praised God, and that press was no “offence” to him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where then was the “offence”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When his wife came to him and said, “Speak a word against God, and die.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When all had been taken from him by the devil, an Eve was reserved for the exercised sufferer, not to console but to tempt her husband.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then where the offence was.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She exaggerated his miseries, and her miseries too with his, and began to persuade him to blaspheme.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But he who was “meek,” because “God had taught him out of His law, and given him rest from the days of adversity;” had “great peace” in his heart as “loving the law of God, and nothing was an offence to him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She was an offence, but not to him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In a word, behold the meek man, behold one taught in the law of God, the eternal law of God I mean.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that law on tables was not yet given to the Jews in the time of Job, but in the hearts of the godly there remained still the eternal law, from which that which was given to the people was copied.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then by the law of God he had “rest given him from the days of adversity,” and “had great peace as loving the law of God,” behold how “meek” he is, and what he answers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Learn hereby what I propose to enquire; who are the meek.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou speakest,” he says, “as one of the foolish women speaketh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we have received good from the hand of the Lord, shall we not bear the evil?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard by an example who the meek are: let us, if we can, define them in words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The meek are they, to whom in all their good deeds, in all the things they do well, nothing is pleasing but God; to whom in all the evils they suffer, God is not displeasing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now, Brethren, attend to this rule, to this pattern; let us stretch ourselves out to it, let us seek for increase, that we may fill it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what does it profit, that we plant, and water, except God shall give the increase?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For neither is he that planteth anything, neither he that watereth; but God that giveth the increase.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give ear, whosoever thou art, that wouldest be “meek,” who wouldest have “rest from the days of adversity, who lovest the law of God,” that there may be “no offence unto thee,” and that thou mayest “have great peace,” that thou mayest “possess the earth, and delight in the multitude of peace;” give ear, whosoever thou art that wouldest be “meek.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whatsoever good thou doest, be not pleased with thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace unto the humble.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then whatever good thou doest, let nought but God be pleasing to thee; whatever evil thou sufferest, let not God be displeasing to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What needest thou more?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do this, and thou shalt live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The days of adversity shall not overwhelm thee; thou shall escape that which is said, “Woe unto the world because of offences.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For to what world is there woe because of offences, but to that of which it is said, “And the world knew Him not?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not to that world of which it is said, “God was in Christ reconciling the world unto Himself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is an evil world, and there is a good world; the evil world, are all the evil men in this world; and the good world, all the good in this world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As we observe frequently with a field.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This field is full: of what?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet we say also, and say truly too, This field is full of chaff.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So with a tree, it is full of fruit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Another says, it is full of leaves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And both he who says it is full of fruit, says true; and he who says it is full of leaves, says true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Neither has the full display of leaves taken away the room for the fruit, nor the full display of the fruit driven off the abundance of leaves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is full of both; but the one the wind searcheth out, the other the husbandman gathereth in.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So therefore when thou dost hear, “Woe unto the world because of offences,” be not afraid; “love the law of God, nothing shall be an offence to thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thy wife comes to thee advising thee to some evil thing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost love her as a wife should be loved; she is one of thy members.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But if thine eye offend thee, if thine hand offend thee, if thy foot offend thee,” thou hast just heard the Gospel, “cut them off, and cast them from thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whosoever he be that is dear to thee, whosoever he be that is held in high estimation by thee, let him be so long of high esteem with thee, so long thy beloved member, as he shall not begin to offend thee, that is, to advise thee to any evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear now how that this is the meaning of “offence.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have brought forward the example of Job and his wife; but there the word “offence” did not occur.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Gospel: when the Lord prophesied of His Passion, Peter began to persuade him not to suffer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Get thee behind Me, Satan, thou art an offence to Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here undoubtedly the Lord who hath given thee an example of life, hath taught thee both what an “offence” is, and how an offence is to be avoided.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Him to whom He had a little while before said, “Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona;” He had shown to be His member.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when he begins to be an offence, He cuts off the member; only He restored the member, and put it into its place again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then will be an “offence” to thee, who shall begin to persuade thee to any evil thing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And here, Beloved, take heed; this takes place for the most part not through any evil will, but through a mistaken good will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy friend who loves thee, and is loved by thee again, thy father, thy brother, thy child, thy wife, sees thee in an evil case, and would have thee do what is evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do I mean by “sees thee in an evil case”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Sees thee in some press of trouble.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This pressure it may be thou art suffering for righteousness’ sake; art suffering it because thou will not give false witness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I would speak merely by way of illustration.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Examples abound; for “woe to the world, because of offences.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, for instance, some powerful person, to cover his rapine and plunder, asks of you the service of a false witness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You refuse: refuse the false oath, lest thou shouldest deny Him that is true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That I may not dwell long on this, he is angry, he is powerful, he oppresses thee: a friend comes who would not have thee in this press of trouble, in this evil case; “I pray thee, do what is told thee; what great matter is it?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then perhaps as Satan with the Lord, “It is written of Thee, He shall give His Angels charge concerning Thee, that Thou dash not Thy foot against a stone.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Perhaps too this friend of thine, because he sees thou art a Christian, wishes to persuade thee out of the Law to do what he thinks you ought to do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Do what the other tells.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do what the other wishes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But it is a lie, it is false.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Well, have you not read, ‘All men are liars’?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now is he an “offence.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is a friend, what will you do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is an eye, he is a hand: “Cut it off, and cast it from thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “cut it off, and cast it from thee”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consent not to him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For members in our body make up unity by consent, by consent they live, by consent are joined together one with the other.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where there is dissent, there is disease, or a sore.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is then one of thy members; thou wilt love him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But he is an offence to thee; “Cut him off, and cast him from thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consent not to him; drive him off from thine ears, it may be he will return amended.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how wilt thou do this that I say, “Cut him off, and cast him from thee,” and so, it may be, amend him? answer me, how thou art going to do it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He wished to persuade thee out of the Law to tell a lie.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he said, “speak.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And perhaps he did not dare to say, “speak a lie;” but thus, “speak what the other wishes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou sayest, “But it is a lie.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he to excuse it, says, “All men are liars.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then do thou, my brother, say against this, “The mouth that lieth slayeth the soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark, it is no light thing thou hast heard, “The mouth that lieth slayeth the soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What can that powerful enemy, who oppresseth me, do to me, that thou pitiest me, and my condition, and wouldest not have me be in this evil case; whereas thou wouldest that I should be evil?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What can that powerful man do to me, and what can he oppress?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He can oppress thy body, thou wilt say: I grant he may oppress it to destruction.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Still how much more mildly does he deal with me, than I should with myself were I to lie!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He kills my flesh; I kill my soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He in his power and anger slays the body; “the mouth that lieth slayeth the soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He slays the body; and die it must, though it should not be slain; but the soul which iniquity slayeth not, the truth receiveth for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Preserve then what thou canst preserve; and let that perish which must perish sometime or other.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast given an answer then, but thou hast not solved the “All men are liars.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Make answer to him to this too, that he may not fancy that he has said anything to persuade to lying, in bringing a testimony out of the Law; so urging thee out of the Law against the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is written in the Law, “Thou shalt not bear false witness;” and it is written in the Law, “All men are liars.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Recur then to that which I just lately suggested, when I defined in words as best I could the “meek” man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is “meek” to whom in all things that he does well, nothing but God is pleasing, and in all the evils which he suffers, God is not displeasing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Make answer then to him who says, Lie, for it is written, “All men are liars:” I will not lie, for it is written, “The mouth that lieth slayeth the soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will not lie, because it is written, “Thou shalt destroy them that speak lying.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will not lie, because it is written, “Thou shalt not bear false witness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Though he whom I displease by the truth harass my body with oppressions, I will give ear to my Lord, “Fear not them which kill the body.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “How then are all men liars?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art not a man, I suppose?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Answer quickly and truly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And O that I may not be a man, that so I may not be a liar.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For see; “God looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any that did understand, and seek after God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are all gone out of the way, they are all together become unprofitable: there is none that doeth good, no not even one.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because they wished to be sons of men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in order that he might deliver them from these iniquities, cure, heal, change, the sons of men; “he gave them power to become the sons of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What marvel then!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye were men, if we were the sons of men; ye were all men, and were liars, for, “All men are liars.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The grace of God came to you, and “gave you power to become the sons of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the voice of My Father saying, “I have said, Ye are gods; and ye are all the children of the Most High.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Since then they are men, and the sons of men, if they are not the children of the Most High, they are liars, for, “all men are liars.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If they are the sons of God, if they have been redeemed by the Saviour’s grace, if purchased with His precious Blood, if born again of water and of the Spirit, if predestinated to the inheritance of heaven, then indeed are they children of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so thereby are gods.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then would a lie have to do with thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Adam was a mere man, Christ, man and God; God, the Creator of all creation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Adam a mere man, the Man Christ, the Mediator with God, the Only Son of the Father, the God-man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, thou, O man, art far from God, and God is far above man; between them the God-man placed Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Acknowledge Christ, and by Him as Man ascend up to God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Being then now reformed, and, if my words have been so blessed, “meek,” let us “hold fast our profession without wavering.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us love the law of God, that we may escape that which is written, “Woe unto the world because of offences.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now I would say a few words about “offences,” of which the world is full, and how it is that offences thicken, pressing troubles abound.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The world is laid waste, the winepress is trodden.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christians, heavenly shoot, ye strangers on the earth, who seek a city in heaven, who long to be associated with the holy Angels; understand that ye have come here on this condition only, that ye should soon depart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are passing on through the world, endeavouring to reach Him who created it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not the lovers of the world, who wish to remain in the world, and yet, whether they will or no, are compelled to move from it; let them not disturb you, let them not deceive nor seduce you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These pressing troubles are not offences.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be ye righteous, and they will be only exercises.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tribulation comes; it will be as ye choose it, either an exercise, or a condemnation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such as it shall find you to be, will it be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tribulation is a fire; does it find thee gold? it takes away the filth: does it find thee chaff? it turns it into ashes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The pressing troubles then which abound are not “offences.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what are “offences”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Those expressions, those words in which we are thus addressed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “See what Christian times bring about;” lo, these are the true offences.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this is said to thee, to this end, that if thou love the world, thou mayest blaspheme Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And this he saith to thee who is thy friend, and counsellor; and so “thine eye.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This he saith to thee who ministereth to thee, and shareth thy labours, and so “thine hand.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This he saith to thee it may be who supporteth thee, who lifteth thee up from a low earthly state; and so “thy foot.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Cast them all aside, cut them off, throw them all away from thee; consent not unto them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Answer such men, as he who was advised to give false witness answered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So do thou answer too; say to the man who saith to thee, “See, it is in Christian times that there are such pressing troubles; that the whole world is laid waste;” answer him, “And this Christ foretold me, before it came to pass.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For wherefore art thou disturbed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thine heart is disturbed by the pressing troubles of the world, as that ship was, in which Christ was asleep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo! what is the cause, stout-hearted man, that thy heart is disturbed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That ship in which Christ is asleep, is the heart in which faith is asleep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what new thing, what new thing, I ask, is told thee, Christian?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In Christian times is the world laid waste, the world is failing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did not thy Lord tell thee, the world shall be laid waste?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did not thy Lord tell thee, the world shall fail?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why when the promise was made, didst thou believe, and art disturbed now, when it is being completed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then the tempest beats furiously against thine heart; beware of shipwreck, awake up Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle says, “that Christ may dwell in your hearts by faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ dwelleth in thee by faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Present faith, is Christ present; waking faith, is Christ awake; slumbering faith, is Christ asleep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Arise and stir thyself; say, “Lord, we perish.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See what the Heathen say to us; and what is worse, what evil Christians say!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Awake up, O Lord, we perish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let thy faith awake, and Christ begins to speak to thee. “‘Why art thou troubled?’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I told thee beforehand of all these things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I foretold them, that when evils came, thou mightest hope for good things, that thou mightest not faint in the evil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wonderest thou that the world is failing?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wonder that the world is grown old.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is as a man who is born, and grows up, and waxes old.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are many complaints in old age; the cough, the rheum, the weakness of the eyes, fretfulness, and weariness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then as when a man is old; he is full of complaints; so is the world old; and is full of troubles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is it a little thing that God hath done for thee, in that in the world’s old age, He hath sent Christ unto thee, that He may renew thee then, when all is failing?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou not know that He notified this in the seed of Abraham?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The seed of Abraham,” says the Apostle, “which is Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He saith not, And to seeds, as of many; but as of One, And to thy seed, which is Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore was there a son born to Abraham in his old age, because in the old age of this world was Christ to come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He came when all things were growing old, and made them new.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As a made, created, perishing thing, the world was now declining to its fall.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It could not but be that it should abound in troubles; He came both to console thee in the midst of present troubles, and to promise thee everlasting rest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Choose not then to cleave to this aged world, and to be unwilling to grow young in Christ, who telleth thee, “The world is perishing, the world is waxing old, the world is failing; is distressed by the heavy breathing of old age.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But do not fear, “Thy youth shall be renewed as the eagle’s.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, they say, in Christian times it is that Rome perishes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Perhaps Rome is not perishing; perhaps she is only scourged, not utterly destroyed; perhaps she is chastened, not brought to nought.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It may be so; Rome will not perish, if the Romans do not perish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And perish they will not if they praise God; perish they will if they blaspheme Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what is Rome, but the Romans?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the question is not of her wood and stones, of her lofty insulated palaces, and all her spacious walls.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All this was made only on this condition that it should fall some other day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When man built it, he laid stone on stone; and when man destroyed it, he removed stone from stone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Man made it, man destroyed it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is any injury done to Rome, because it is said, “She is falling”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No, not to Rome, but to her builder perhaps.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do we then its builder any injury, because we say, Rome is falling, which Romulus built?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This world itself will be burnt with fire, which God built.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But neither does what man has made fall to ruin, except when God wills it; nor what God has made, except when He wills.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if the work of man fall not without God’s will, how can God’s work fall by the will of man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet God both made the world that was one day to fall for thee; and therefore made He thee as one who was one day to die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Man himself, the city’s ornament, man himself, the city’s inhabitant, ruler, governor, comes on this condition that he may go, is born on this condition that he may die, entered into the world on this condition that he may pass away; “Heaven and earth shall pass away:” what wonder then if some time or other there should be an end of a single city?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet peradventure the city’s end is not come now; yet some time or other come it will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why does Rome perish amid the sacrifices of Christians?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why was her mother Troy burnt amid the sacrifices of Heathens?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The gods in whom the Romans have placed all their hope, yea the Roman gods in whom the Heathen Romans placed their hope, removed from the flames of Troy to found Rome.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These very gods of Rome were originally the gods of Troy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Troy was burnt, and Æneas took the fugitive gods; yea rather himself a fugitive he took away these senseless gods.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they could be carried by the fugitive; but they could not flee away themselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And coming with these gods into Italy, with these false gods, he founded Rome.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is too long to go through the whole story; yet would I briefly mention what their own writings contain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: An author of theirs well known to all speaks thus; “As I have received the account, the Trojans who under the guidance of Æneas were wandering about as fugitives without any settled abode, originally built and inhabited Rome.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So they had their gods with them, they builded Rome in Latium, and there they placed the gods to be worshipped, which before were worshipped in Troy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Juno is introduced by their poet, incensed against Æneas and the fugitive Trojans, saying,

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “A race of wandering slaves abhorred by me,

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With prosperous passage cuts the Tuscan sea,

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To fruitful Italy their course they steer,

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And for their vanquished gods, design new temples

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now when these vanquished gods were carried into Italy, was it as a protecting deity, or as a presage of their future fall?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Love” therefore “the law of God, and nothing shall be an offence to you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We pray you, we beseech you, we exhort you; be meek, sympathize with the suffering, bear the weak; and on this occasion of the concourse of so many strangers, and needy, and suffering people, let your hospitality and your good works abound.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let but Christians do what Christ enjoineth, and so will the Heathen blaspheme only to their own hurt.
